prompts.abstract: A rectangular block with a slightly rounded top and bottom edges. The block has a uniform thickness throughout its length and width.
prompts.description: A rectangular block with a slightly rounded top and bottom edges. The block has a uniform thickness throughout its length and width.
prompts.beginner: Design a small rectangular block with a length of 0.75 units, a width of 0.375 units, and a height of 0.0469 units. This block will be used as a basic building element in the final assembly.
prompts.intermediate: Start by creating a new coordinate system aligned with the default axes. Draw a rectangular sketch on the first face, ensuring the proportions match the intended dimensions. After completing the sketch, scale it down to fit the desired size. Transform the scaled sketch into a three-dimensional object by rotating and translating it to the correct position. Extrude the sketch to create a solid rectangular block with a specified height. The final dimensions of the block are 0.75 units in length, 0.375 units in width, and 0.0469 units in height. This block will serve as a foundational component that can be integrated with other parts to form a larger assembly.
prompts.expert: Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees and a translation vector also set to zero. Begin by drawing the first two-dimensional sketch on the first face. In this sketch, draw the first line from the origin at (0.0, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.0), then draw the second line from (0.75, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.375). Continue with the third line from (0.75, 0.375) to (0.0, 0.375), and complete the loop with the fourth line from (0.0, 0.375) back to the origin at (0.0, 0.0). After completing the sketch, apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch to adjust its size. Transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into three dimensions by rotating it using the Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and translating it using the translation vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. Next, extrude the sketch 0.0469 units along the direction of the normal without extruding in the opposite direction. Finally, create a new solid body from the extruded sketch. The final dimensions of the rectangular block will be 0.75 units in length, 0.375 units in width, and 0.0469 units in height.
prompts.all_level_data:
<level1>
Create a new coordinate system with Euler angles set to zero degrees and a translation vector also set to zero. Begin by drawing the first two-dimensional sketch on the first face. In this sketch, draw the first line from the origin at (0.0, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.0), then draw the second line from (0.75, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.375). Continue with the third line from (0.75, 0.375) to (0.0, 0.375), and complete the loop with the fourth line from (0.0, 0.375) back to the origin at (0.0, 0.0). After completing the sketch, apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch to adjust its size. Transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into three dimensions by rotating it using the Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0] and translating it using the translation vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0]. Next, extrude the sketch 0.0469 units along the direction of the normal without extruding in the opposite direction. Finally, create a new solid body from the extruded sketch. The final dimensions of the rectangular block will be 0.75 units in length, 0.375 units in width, and 0.0469 units in height.
</level1>

<level2>
Start by creating a new coordinate system aligned with the default axes. Draw a rectangular sketch on the first face, ensuring the proportions match the intended dimensions. After completing the sketch, scale it down to fit the desired size. Transform the scaled sketch into a three-dimensional object by rotating and translating it to the correct position. Extrude the sketch to create a solid rectangular block with a specified height. The final dimensions of the block are 0.75 units in length, 0.375 units in width, and 0.0469 units in height. This block will serve as a foundational component that can be integrated with other parts to form a larger assembly.
</level2>

<level3>
Design a small rectangular block with a length of 0.75 units, a width of 0.375 units, and a height of 0.0469 units. This block will be used as a basic building element in the final assembly.
</level3>
prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Rectangular Block

#### Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler Angles to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].
- Set the Translation Vector to [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].

#### Draw the First 2D Sketch
- **Face 1:**
  - **Loop 1:**
    - **Line 1:** Draw a line from (0.0, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.0).
    - **Line 2:** Draw a line from (0.75, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.375).
    - **Line 3:** Draw a line from (0.75, 0.375) to (0.0, 0.375).
    - **Line 4:** Draw a line from (0.0, 0.375) to (0.0, 0.0).

#### Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch.

#### Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the sketch using the Euler Angles [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].
- Translate the sketch using the Translation Vector [0.0, 0.0, 0.0].

#### Extrude the 2D Sketch to Generate the 3D Model
- Extrude the sketch 0.0469 units in the direction of the normal.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction of the normal.

#### Integrate the Current Part with the Previous Body
- Create a new solid body from the extruded sketch.

#### Final Dimensions
- **Length:** 0.75
- **Width:** 0.375
- **Height:** 0.0469

</part_1>
